opp.ais_why:
• Scott Fields, the sole procurement lead, sized the ANA pricing as a discrete line item on the March 24 2026 commercial call. The action item was to quote the analytics module standalone, ANA standalone, and combined pricing, which means the AI agent module has reached the budget conversation. AI importance is rated Important.
• Scott described Gen AI as an enabler to prove procurement value early. He engaged the AutoClass AI engine to convert messy NetSuite and spreadsheet data into analysis-ready spend, the Merlin Insights conversational layer in Microsoft Teams, and the Anna autonomous negotiation agent for tail-spend automation. Demo received a direct positive reaction recorded as really good.
• Greenfield procurement function with only 30 percent of contracts in repository, scattered spreadsheets and NetSuite limited to bill payments. Three completed calls in February and March 2026 with Scott as sole decision maker. Budget constraints may require starting with analytics before adding Anna, but both modules are scoped and priced. The Hungry call rests on a tail-spend automation that pays for itself in year one.

metrics.narrative: Scott articulated the business problem on 2026-02-18 discovery call: he is a new VP of Procurement tasked with optimizing all procurement processes, with initial priority on intake, eProcurement, and spend analytics. He stated the project is unfunded and set a tight budget constraint of $60K–70K for a one-year modular entry, avoiding multi-year commitments, because he must demonstrate ROI to secure ongoing funding. On 2026-03-24 demo Scott said 'I'm solo right now. I'm not gonna have a team anytime soon,' indicating he is building the function from scratch with limited resources. Next Step 2026-03-01 notes 'scope largely Spend / Supplier with limited budget' and 'trying to gauge investment.' However, no quantified metrics were captured: no baseline spend under management, no current process cycle time, no error rate, no target savings or ROI figure. The qualitative pain is clear (new procurement leader, unfunded, must prove value), but the absence of hard numbers makes it difficult to anchor ROI discussions or justify the investment to an Economic Buyer. Quantifying the value case is the critical next step to move this deal forward.

Scott attended a Zycus event in Atlanta on 2026-03-13 and provided 'positive feedback' (Next Step 2026-03-17), then had a demo on 2026-03-24, but no follow-on use-case validation, workshop, or deeper demo has occurred since. A formal decision-criteria review with Scott and the Economic Buyer is needed to confirm fit and lock the evaluation.

He engaged the AutoClass AI engine to convert messy NetSuite and spreadsheet data into analysis-ready spend, the Merlin Insights conversational layer in Microsoft Teams, and the Anna autonomous negotiation agent for tail-spend automation. • Greenfield procurement function with only 30 percent of contracts in repository, scattered spreadsheets and NetSuite limited to bill payments. Three completed calls in February and March 2026 with Scott as sole decision maker. Budget constraints may require starting with analytics before adding Anna, but both modules are scoped and priced. The Hungry call rests on a tail-spend automation that pays for itself in year one.

On 2026-04-23, Scott mentioned a 'new team member' but never identified them, and the proposed follow-up demo never happened. Recommend: when Scott re-engages, ask to meet the new team member, map IT/Finance stakeholders who would use or approve the platform (e.g., FP&A lead Scott MacKenzie, Finance Director Alex Shipp, IT leads Seth Wegner or Joseph Keena per account contacts), and request an intro to the CFO or COO for budget validation. Multi-threading builds resilience against single-contact ghosting and accelerates executive buy-in. If Scott resists multi-threading, it is a red flag that he may lack internal support or urgency.
